title: Sales & Booking Specialist
description:
***To proceed with the application process, please upload your resume into this Google form:
 and select the role.***



Role Summary

As a Sales & Booking Specialist, you are responsible for converting guest inquiries into confirmed bookings across Airbnb, VRBO, Zillow, and direct channels.



Your core objective is to maximize conversion rate, occupancy, and revenue through fast response, strong sales communication, and effective follow-up.



This is a performance-driven sales role, not just customer service. You are expected to actively guide guests through the booking process and drive results.



Work Schedule

 Standard: 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM Pacific Time

 Flexibility required: occasional shifts in Philippines Standard Time based on business needs



Key Responsibilities

1. Lead Conversion & Sales



 Respond to guest inquiries across Airbnb, VRBO, Zillow, and other platforms in a timely manner

 Identify guest needs and recommend suitable properties

 Use persuasive communication to highlight property features, amenities, and location advantages

 Address objections and remove booking friction

 Proactively follow up with leads to maximize conversion



2. Revenue & Listing Performance



 Take ownership of booking performance for assigned listings

 Monitor key metrics:

 Traffic

 Inquiry volume

 Conversion rate

 Occupancy



 Identify underperforming listings and recommend improvements (pricing, photos, positioning)

 Collaborate with marketing and pricing teams on optimization strategies



3. Booking Management & Guest Coordination



 Guide guests through the booking process and ensure all information is clearly communicated

 Coordinate with internal teams (operations, cleaning, maintenance) to ensure readiness

 Maintain high response rate and fast response time

 Assist with booking modifications and guest coordination



4. Performance Tracking & Reporting



 Track and report:

 Lead volume

 Inquiry-to-booking conversion rate

 Occupancy rate

 Revenue per listing



 Provide insights and recommendations to improve booking performance



Description



Month2Month is a fast-growing hospitality and property management company focused on maximizing occupancy and delivering exceptional guest experiences.

As a  Sales & Booking Specialist, you will play a critical role in driving revenue by converting guest inquiries into confirmed bookings across multiple platforms including Airbnb, VRBO, and Zillow.

This role is highly execution-driven and performance-focused. You will work directly on the front line of our booking operations, responding to leads, guiding guests through the reservation process, and ensuring listings perform at a high level.

This is not a passive customer service role. Success in this position requires a strong sales mindset, fast response time, attention to detail, and the ability to actively follow up and close bookings.

You will work closely with cross-functional teams including operations, pricing, and marketing to continuously improve listing performance and maximize occupancy.



Required Qualifications



 Bachelor’s degree or above

 Experience in property management, short-term rental, hospitality, or related industry

 Fluent in English with strong written communication skills

 Strong sales mindset with ability to convert leads into bookings

 Ability to multitask and manage multiple inquiries simultaneously

 Strong sense of urgency and responsiveness

 Comfortable working in a fast-paced, performance-driven environment

 Basic proficiency with spreadsheets and digital tools



Preferred Qualifications

 2+ years of experience in short-term rental, OTA platforms, or sales roles

 Experience with Airbnb, VRBO, Zillow, or similar platforms

 Proven track record in lead conversion or sales performance

 Familiarity with PMS systems and booking workflows

 Ability to analyze listing performance and identify improvement opportunities

 Experience working with remote and cross-functional teams

 and select the role.***

title: Construction Estimator / Takeoff Specialist (Ongoing Per-Project Work)
description:
We are looking for an experienced construction estimator / takeoff specialist for ongoing per-project work.



Project size is typically small to mid-size commercial (5,000 – 10,000 sq ft), with occasional larger scopes.



Responsibilities:

• Perform accurate quantity takeoffs from construction drawings

• Read and interpret civil, architectural, and structural plans

* Concrete (slabs, footings)

* Basic sitework (grading areas, paving)

* Framing / structural elements

Requirements:

• Proven experience with construction takeoffs (commercial preferred)

• Ability to read plans without hand-holding

• Experience with software such as PlanSwift, Bluebeam, or similar

We will start with a small paid test project. If successful, this will turn into consistent ongoing work.



We are looking for someone reliable, fast, and detail-oriented — not beginners.



To apply, include:



1. Sample takeoff (Excel)

2. Marked-up drawings

3. What software you use

4. Your typical turnaround time



Applications without samples will NOT be considered.

title: Remote Primary Care Nurse (RN)
description:
About Mulberry Health



Mulberry Health is a forward-thinking healthcare provider dedicated to delivering exceptional primary care services remotely. We are committed to leveraging technology to provide accessible, high-quality care to our patients. We are expanding our team and looking for three dedicated and skilled nurses from the Philippines to join us in our mission.



IMPORTANT — In-Person Training Requirement



Our CEO, Joseph Furtado, will be in Manila from April 12 to April 18, 2026. Candidates who are hired must be available to attend a mandatory in-person training session in Manila during that week. This is a paid training opportunity and a chance to meet our leadership team face-to-face before beginning your remote role. Please only apply if you can commit to being in Manila for this training period.



Job Summary



We are seeking experienced and compassionate Remote Primary Care Nurses to provide comprehensive virtual nursing care and back-office support. The ideal candidates will be responsible for a combination of clinical and administrative tasks, ensuring our patients receive the best possible care and our operations run smoothly. This is a full-time, remote position for candidates based in the Philippines.



Key Responsibilities



Primary Care Nursing (70%):

- Conduct virtual patient consultations via phone and video to assess patient conditions, provide nursing care, and offer patient education.

- Monitor and document patient vital signs, symptoms, and health progress in the EMR system.

- Collaborate with a multidisciplinary team of physicians, specialists, and other healthcare professionals to coordinate patient care.

- Develop and implement individualized patient care plans.

- Assist patients with medication management and adherence.



MA Back-Office Responsibilities (30%):

- Manage patient appointments, including scheduling, rescheduling, and sending reminders.

- Handle administrative tasks such as processing medical billing, managing patient records, and ensuring data accuracy.

- Transcribe physician's notes and update patient charts in the EMR system.

- Assist with prior authorizations and other insurance-related tasks.



Required Qualifications:

- Active and unrestricted Registered Nurse (RN) license in the Philippines (PRC License).

- Minimum of 2 years of clinical experience in a hospital or primary care setting.

- Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English.

- Reliable high-speed internet connection (minimum 150 Mbps) and a quiet, professional home office setup.

- Strong organizational skills, attention to detail, and the ability to work independently.

- Must be available Mon-Fri, 9am-5pm Arizona time (MST/MDT).

- Must be available for in-person training in Manila from April 12-18, 2026.



Preferred Qualifications:

- Previous experience in a remote nursing or telehealth role.

- Proficiency with EMR/EHR systems, with a strong preference for eClinicalWorks (ECW). Experience with Epic, Athena, or similar platforms is also valued.

- US Registered Nurse (USRN) license is a strong advantage but not required.

- BLS and/or ACLS certifications are highly desirable.



Compensation and Benefits:

- Competitive hourly rate: $5.00 - $8.00 USD per hour (commensurate with experience)

- 15 days paid time off (PTO) annually

- 10 paid US holidays

- Opportunities for ongoing training and career advancement

title: Dispute Manager for DTC Men's Hair Brand
description:
Voke is a fast-growing direct-to-consumer men's hair wellness brand based in the US. We sell a natural DHT-blocking supplement (Pumpkin Seed Oil softgels) that helps men stop hair loss without the side effects of prescription drugs. We run lean, move fast, and operate fully remote via Slack.



We're looking for a Senior Dispute Manager to own our chargeback and payment dispute operations end-to-end. This is a full-time role with real ownership, you won't be filing tickets, you'll be building and running the process.



What You'll Own



Managing and resolving all chargebacks and payment disputes across our payment processors

Building and maintaining dispute response templates, evidence packages, and win-rate tracking

Monitoring chargeback ratios and flagging risk before it becomes a threshold issue

Working directly with our payment processors to represent disputes and protect revenue

Identifying patterns in dispute reasons and feeding insights back to the team (fulfillment, CS, ad claims)

Requirements



Proven experience managing payment disputes and chargebacks — this is non-negotiable

Deep hands-on experience with PayPal disputes (required)

Strong understanding of chargeback reason codes, representment strategy, and evidence documentation

Comfortable working async and remotely across time zones

Detail-oriented and proactive, you catch problems before they escalate

Bonus



Experience with Payarc is a strong plus

What to Expect



Full-time, remote position

All communication runs through Slack

You'll be working directly with a small, high-performance team

Fast feedback loops — decisions get made quickly here



To Apply, kindly fill-out the application form below:





Only those who fill-out the form will be screened for the position

title: Creative Strategist for DTC Brands / Dropshipping
description:
We’re Hiring a Creative Strategist (DTC / Dropshipping)



Flexible Schedule • Performance-Driven • Fast-Moving Creative Team



Hey Creative legends,



We’re an ambitious DTC e-commerce brand scaling aggressively across the UK and Germany. We’ve built past 8 figures without a dedicated Creative Strategist—but with Meta’s Andromeda update reshaping the game, it’s finally time to bring in someone who can own our creative direction.



If you know how to turn attention into conversions, break down angles and hooks, and build ads that actually perform, this role is for you.



This is not just a brief-writing job.

This is a strategic role that drives the entire direction of our creative output—concepts, scripts, research, testing, iteration, insights. If you love storytelling, performance ads, UGC structure, and analyzing what truly works, you’ll thrive here.



What You’ll Do



-Own creative strategy across UK & Germany

-Research hooks, angles, and trends using Meta Ads Library, TikTok, YouTube, and competitor analysis

-Build creative concepts and iterate based on real Meta performance data

- Master VSL copywriting, hooks, and story-driven ad frameworks

-Write clear, actionable creative briefs for editors, designers, and UGC creators

-Manage the creative testing pipeline:

Brainstorm ? Produce ? Test ? Analyze ? Iterate

-Lead and collaborate with a team of video editors

-Work closely with the Media Buyer to analyze CTR, CPC, CPA, CVR, and ROAS

-Use AI tools to speed up research, ideation, scripting, and testing

-Stay ahead of Meta trends (especially post-Andromeda)

-Organize creatives by angle, format, and funnel stage inside Notion / Google Drive



You’re a Great Fit If You:



-Have 2+ years in creative strategy, performance marketing, or DTC/dropshipping

-Understand direct-response psychology and why people click + buy

-Can explain why an ad works—and where the hook or angle comes from

-Are skilled in concept ideation, copywriting, and scripting

-Can manage creative teams and ensure fast, high-quality output

-Thrive in fast-paced, test-heavy environments with multiple product cycles

-Communicate clearly and professionally in English



Weekly Deliverables



-Creative briefs

-Hook & angle research

-VSL scripts

-Creative analysis with next-step recommendations

-Rapid testing and iteration (quality matters, but speed matters too)



???? Salary & Benefits



-$1,500 USD/month

-Performance bonuses tied to creative results

-Long-term role with strong growth potential

-Flexible working hours



???? How to Apply



Please submit your application using the ClickUp Form below:





Applications submitted outside the form will not be reviewed.

title: Full-Stack Developer -- React + Supabase SaaS Application
description:
SaaS company looking for a developer to maintain and improve our web application built with React, TypeScript, and Supabase.



We are building SaaS platform for our clients using AI & Claude Code.

We have built a Master AI Agent that has access to the code base and we currently use it to escalate issues.



You will be the bridge between our client's feedback and our AI-assisted development workflow. Your day-to-day will look like this:



- Receive bug reports and feature requests from the client via Slack (screenshots, voice messages, written feedback)

- Work hand in hand with our Master AI in order to create different issues on github. Sometimes the AI itself might not have all the context needed to identify exactly what needs to fixed. This is where your understand of the project will be precious

- Have claude fix the issues inside github (simply by 

- Merge PRs one by one and see that issues/improvements are effective.





Tech stack you will work with daily:

- React 18 with TypeScript

- Supabase (PostgreSQL, Row Level Security, Edge Functions, Realtime)

- Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui components

- React Query for data fetching

- Vercel for deployment

- GitHub for version control and issue tracking



You do NOT need to be a senior architect. You need to be someone who can understand the platform deeply and all the functionalities, how things connect, etc. The AI partner handles detailed implementation specs -- your job is to execute reliably and verify thoroughly.



What makes this role unique: you will work directly with an AI CTO agent that has full context of the codebase. You discuss problems with it, it helps you diagnose issues, you create GitHub issues together, and you implement the solutions. This is a new way of working and we need someone comfortable with that.



Requirements:

- Strong working knowledge of React and TypeScript

- Experience with Supabase or PostgreSQL

- Comfortable reading and navigating an existing codebase (not just building from scratch)

- Familiar with Git and GitHub workflows (branches, PRs, issues)

- Attention to detail when testing -- you are the last line before the client sees changes

- Good written English for communicating issues and status updates

- Self-motivated, does not need hand-holding once given clear specs



Nice to have:

- Experience with Tailwind CSS

- Familiarity with Vercel deployments

- Previous QA or bug triage experience



Disclaimer: client feedback volume varries every week. Some week might not receive feedback at all. Some weeks might be full of work

title: Media buyer
description:
Media buyer – Pressure Washing Marketing Agency







Part-time / Remote · 







About the role







We are a marketing agency that runs meta ads for pressure washing businesses across the US. We currently manage 14 clients and are growing. We are looking for a reliable media buyer to handle the day-to-day execution of our clients' ad campaigns.







You do NOT need to write ad copy, scripts, or strategy. We provide all the scripts .Your job is to edit the video creatives through the voiceovers we provide and footage we have and upload the creatives to the ad account of the client and launch the ads, monitor performance based on how many leads we are getting and report daily.







This is a flexible remote role. Workload varies day to day — some days are monitoring, others involve editing and launching. Estimated 5–8 hours per day on average across all clients







What you will do







Edit video ad creatives for each client using raw footage and scripts we provide (captions, cuts, music, branding)

Launch ads in Meta Ads Manager using the creatives you edited

Check each client's ads every day and monitor performance

Edit and update live campaigns based on instructions (audience, budget, copy)

Report back to the agency owner daily with a simple performance update

Flag any issues, disapprovals, or drops in performance immediately

title: Short?Form Video Editor (Reels/Shorts/TikTok) – Finance Brand – Long?Term
description:
We are a fast?growing finance/markets education brand (Bull Market Blueprint – BMB) creating daily content for Instagram, YouTube Shorts, and TikTok. We’re looking for 1–2 reliable short?form video editors from the Philippines for a long?term role focused on simple but well executed reel cutdowns from long form content.



This is NOT a one?off gig. We want someone who can grow with us and edit content every week.



Responsibilities

- Take long?form videos (talking?head finance/business content, screen recordings, webinars) and turn them into 20–60 second clips.

- Add clean, readable captions optimized for mobile (we provide references + templates).

- Add basic pattern interrupts: jump cuts, punch?ins, zooms, simple sound emphasis, light B?roll when needed.

- Export in vertical 9:16 format (Reels/Shorts/TikTok ready).

- Work in batches (for example, 10–20 clips from one long video).

- Deliver on time consistently and be responsive on chat during agreed hours.



What this job is NOT

- Not heavy After Effects or complex motion graphics.

- Not cinematic edits or full YouTube documentaries.

- Not random projects; this is ongoing, systemized work.



Requirements

- Proven experience editing short?form vertical content (Reels/Shorts/TikTok). Please have links ready.

- Strong sense of pacing for educational / talking?head videos.

- Good English comprehension; you must catch obvious grammar/spelling mistakes in captions.

- Comfortable following a preset/template so you can edit quickly and consistently.

- Stable internet connection and a decent computer for video editing.

- Software: Adobe Premiere Pro, DaVinci Resolve, or Final Cut Pro (CapCut can be a plus but not as the only tool).

- Experience editing finance, business, or coaching/creator content.

- Familiarity with YouTube/IG/TikTok best practices for hooks, text size, etc.



Work Schedule & Volume

- We are flexible on exact hours as long as deadlines are hit.

- Expect 30–60 shorts per month to start (20–60 sec each), with potential to increase if we’re a good fit.

- Long?term role: we are looking for someone who wants stable, ongoing work.



Salary / Compensation

- Please propose your expected MONTHLY salary in PHP for:

- Part?time: ~20–30 hours/week, 30–60 shorts per month.

- We are open to performance?based increases after the first 1–3 months based on reliability, speed, and quality.



How to Apply

1. Make sure your Onlinejobs.ph profile is complete with your work history and skills.

2. Fill out this short application form (required): 


 

3. In your message here on Onlinejobs.ph, include:

- Subject line: “Short?Form Editor – BMB”

- 2–3 sentences about your experience with Reels/Shorts/TikTok

- Your expected monthly salary (PHP) for part?time



Only applicants who complete the Typeform and share portfolio links will be considered.



We will shortlist candidates and invite them to a small PAID test (1–2 short videos) so both sides can see if it’s a fit before we start a trial month.

title: Full-Time 3D Hard Surface Artist — Sci-Fi & Military Vehicles (Game-Ready Assets)
description:
We are building a 3D asset business selling game-ready vehicle packs on platforms like Fab (Epic Games), CGTrader, and TurboSquid. We are looking for a skilled hard surface 3D artist to join full-time and produce sci-fi and military vehicles, props, and modular environment pieces.



You will receive detailed reference images and a production schedule for each asset pack. Your job is to model, UV unwrap, texture, rig (where needed), render, and export each model to strict quality standards. Every asset must be game-ready, PBR-compatible, and delivered in multiple file formats.

This is a long-term position. If you produce quality work on time, there is room for pay increases as revenue grows.



WHAT YOU WILL BE DOING

• Modeling hard surface vehicles, weapons, props, and environment kits from provided reference images

• Creating clean, optimized topology suitable for real-time game engines (UE5, Unity)

• UV unwrapping with efficient, non-overlapping layouts and consistent texel density

• Texturing with PBR Metallic-Roughness workflow in Substance Painter, Quixel Mixer, or equivalent

• Creating 3 LOD levels per model (LOD0 full detail, LOD1 50% reduction, LOD2 25%)

• Exporting to multiple formats: FBX, OBJ, BLEND (mandatory); MAX, Maya (if available)

• Producing all required renders: hero shots, multi-angle, wireframe overlays, UV layout screenshots, textured vs. untextured comparisons

• Creating 15-second turntable videos (MP4, 1080p) for each model

• Basic rigging and animation where specified (turret rotation, wheel spin, walk cycles, hatch open/close)

• Maintaining consistent design language, naming conventions, and folder structures across all packs



REQUIRED SKILLS

• 3+ years experience in hard surface 3D modeling (vehicles, weapons, mechanical objects)

• Strong topology skills — clean quads/tris, proper edge flow, no n-gons, manifold meshes

• PBR texturing experience — Substance Painter or Quixel Mixer (Metallic-Roughness workflow)

• UV unwrapping — manual layout, efficient use of 0-1 UV space, consistent texel density

• LOD creation — ability to produce optimized lower-detail versions that maintain silhouette quality

• Blender proficiency (primary tool) — modeling, UV, rendering (Cycles/EEVEE), export

• FBX/OBJ export experience — understanding of scale, transforms, and engine compatibility

• Rendering skills — studio lighting, clean presentation renders, turntable animations

• Basic rigging — simple mechanical rigs (rotating turrets, opening hatches, spinning wheels)



PREFERRED (NOT REQUIRED)

• Experience with Unreal Engine 5 (material setup, demo scenes)

• Experience with Unity HDRP

• Familiarity with 3ds Max or Maya

• Experience selling on Fab, CGTrader, TurboSquid, or similar marketplaces

• Concept art or design skills (ability to interpret loose references into detailed models)



SOFTWARE

• Required: Blender, Substance Painter (or Quixel Mixer)

• Preferred: 3ds Max, Maya, Marmoset Toolbag, ZBrush (for detail sculpting)

What We Provide

• Detailed reference images for every model

• A clear production schedule with weekly/monthly milestones

• Technical specifications document with exact quality standards

•  Creative direction and feedback on each deliverable

• Long-term, stable work with growth potential



TO APPLY

Please include in your application:

 1. Your portfolio — show hard surface work (vehicles, weapons, mechanical models). If you have marketplace listings, link them.

 2. Software you use — list your primary tools

 3. Your experience level — years of hard surface modeling, any marketplace/game studio experience

 4. Availability — can you start within 1-2 weeks? Can you commit full-time (40 hrs/week)?

 5. A short message about why you’re a good fit for this role

Do NOT apply if you primarily do character/organic modeling. This role is 100% hard surface.

title: Wound Care Billing Expert
description:
We are seeking a highly experienced medical billing specialist with deep expertise in wound care billing to support and optimize our revenue cycle. This is not a general billing role — we are specifically looking for someone who understands the clinical, coding, and reimbursement nuances of wound care services.



Our organization works with providers delivering advanced wound care treatments, and we need a professional who can ensure maximum reimbursement, compliance, and clean claim submission.

 We are seeking an experienced wound care billing specialist with deep expertise in Medicare Part B and commercial insurance billing for advanced wound care services. This role goes beyond basic billing—we need someone who understands the nuances of coding and reimbursement for debridement, skin substitutes (Q-codes/CTPs), NPWT, and E/M optimization, including proper modifier usage and documentation requirements for medical necessity. Responsibilities include claim submission, denial management, AR follow-up, and identifying opportunities to improve reimbursement and reduce inefficiencies. The ideal candidate has 3+ years of direct wound care billing experience, strong knowledge of CMS guidelines and LCD policies, and a proven track record of increasing collections and reducing denials. CPC or similar certification is preferred. Please include a brief summary of your relevant experience, examples of results achieved, and your rate.

StreamSmile™ is a fast-growing e-commerce brand specializing in premium portable smart displays — including portable TVs, digital calendars, and digital photo frames. We are a direct-to-consumer brand scaling rapidly and are currently migrating our store from Shopify to WooCommerce.

You will work directly with the founder to help maintain and improve our WooCommerce store, make updates to product pages, build and test sales funnels, and ensure our store is always running smoothly.

title: Credit Dispute Specialist VA (Remote | Part-Time) Credit Repair & Funding Company | USA-Based | Philippines Team
description:
About This Role



We're a growing US-based credit repair and business funding company that helps clients remove negative items, rebuild their credit, and qualify for cars, homes, and business funding. We use Credit Repair Cloud (CRC) to manage all client accounts and dispute letters.

We're looking for a detail-oriented, reliable Dispute Specialist to join our remote team full-time. This is a core operations role, you are the person who actually delivers results for our clients. If you're sharp, organized, and take pride in doing things right the first time, this role is for you.



What You'll Do



1. Manage active client accounts inside Credit Repair Cloud

2. Pull and analyze credit reports across all 3 bureaus (Equifax, Experian, TransUnion)

3. Identify and categorize negative items: collections, late payments, charge-offs, inquiries, public records

4. Draft and send dispute letters to credit bureaus using CRC templates

5. Track bureau response deadlines and log all results (deleted, verified, updated)

6. Escalate disputes to Round 2/3 strategies (debt validation, method of verification, goodwill letters)

7. Update client records with status notes after every round

8. Send weekly status updates to the Client Success team

9. Follow all internal SOPs and FCRA compliance guidelines





What We're Looking For



1. Strong written English — you'll be drafting professional letters

2. High attention to detail — mistakes here affect real people's financial lives

3. Organized and self-managed — you own your client list without being micromanaged

4. Comfortable working inside CRMs and web-based platforms

5. Reliable internet connection and a quiet, professional home setup

6. Available to work US Eastern or Central time zone hours (full overlap required)



Important Bonus 



Full experience with Credit Repair Cloud and Go High Level

Knowledge of FCRA, FCBA, or US credit laws

Background in credit repair, collections, or financial services





What You'll Get



1. Starting pay: $5–$6/hour USD (~$400–$650/month part-time)

2. Performance raise to $7-8/hour after 60 days based on quality and output

3. Part-time, 10-25 hours/week — stable, long-term role

4. Full training provided on our SOP, CRC system, and dispute strategies

5. 100% remote — work from home

6. Paid via Wise or direct transfer, monthly





How to Apply

Send the following to 



Your resume (PDF)

A short paragraph (3–5 sentences) explaining why you'd be great at this role

Answer this question: "What would you do if a dispute you sent in Round 1 came back as 'Verified' by the bureau?"



Applications without all 3 items will not be reviewed.

title: Property Operations Coordinator
description:
Remote Property Operations Coordinator (AU Short-Term Rentals)

Philippines-based | Full-time | AEST-aligned



Join One of Australia’s Largest Short-Term Rental Businesses



This is a rare long-term opportunity to work directly with one of Australia’s most established short-term rental operators — supporting a growing portfolio of up to 800 properties across NSW.



Staff Connect Australia is building a high-performing offshore team, and we’re looking for a proactive, detail-driven Property Operations Coordinator to become one of our foundational hires. What You’ll Be Doing

- Respond to enquiries and issues via Airbnb, 
, direct bookings

Admin, Reporting & Growth

- Prepare weekly/monthly reports using spreadsheets or dashboards

- Help draft and improve SOPs, internal docs, and guest manuals

- Identify workflow gaps and suggest smarter processes or automations



As the business grows, your role will grow with it — including pricing, owner reporting, SOP ownership, and broader operational oversight.



Who We’re Looking For



You’re someone who:

- Is confident on the phone and professional with both guests and property owners

- Thrives in fast-paced, customer-facing environments

- Is organised, proactive, and solutions-focused

- Can juggle multiple tools and tasks without being micromanaged

- Takes ownership and actually cares about doing things properly



Skills & Experience



Required:

- Excellent spoken & written English

- Stable internet connection

- Experience (or fast learning ability) with Aircall, Homhero

- Familiarity with Airbnb, 
, and direct booking sites

Preferred (but not mandatory)

We’re open to candidates with less experience if you show initiative, strong communication skills, and the right mindset.



Tools You’ll Use

What You’ll Get:

- Paid training & onboarding

- Direct mentorship from leadership

- Long-term career growth in a scaling business

- Exposure to end-to-end Australian STR operations



A role where initiative, ownership, and ideas are genuinely valued

- Experience preparing Canadian T1 personal income tax returns

- Experience using ProTax software (by Intuit/Wolters Kluwer) — this is non-negotiable

- Understanding of Canadian tax rules (employment income, rental income, self-employment, RRSP, capital gains, etc.)

- Ability to start within 48 hours

- Reliable internet connection



NICE TO HAVE:

- T2 corporate tax experience (we have T2 work coming after T1 season)

- Experience with CRA Auto-fill My Return (AFR)

- Multiple years of Canadian tax prep experience



HOW YOU WILL WORK:

- You will log into our ProTax system via Intuit login (cloud-based access)

- All client data stays in our system — you work directly in our environment

- No software to install — just log in and start

- Our in-house reviewer will review and EFILE all returns

- You focus on preparation only



WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR:

- Someone who can prep 5-8 T1 returns per day

- Accuracy and attention to detail

- Responsive communication (we need to move fast)



TO APPLY — Please include in your application:

1. How many Canadian T1 returns you have prepared

2. How long you have used ProTax specifically

3. Your availability to start

4. Your expected rate (per return or monthly)

title: Canadian Payroll Specialist — QBO & Gusto — Remote
description:
Canadian bookkeeping firm seeking a reliable, detail-oriented payroll specialist to handle end-to-end payroll processing for multiple clients. You will be preparing payroll for review — not just running it blindly. This role requires someone who catches errors before they happen, communicates proactively, and takes ownership of the process.



REQUIREMENTS (MUST HAVE):

- Experience processing Canadian payroll for multiple clients

- Proficiency in QuickBooks Online (QBO) payroll — you must be comfortable posting payroll journal entries and reconciling in QBO

- Understanding of Canadian payroll rules: CPP, EI, income tax deductions, T4s, ROEs

- Ability to extract and compile time data from various sources (timesheets, time tracking apps, spreadsheets) and prepare it accurately for payroll processing

- Prepare payroll for review — you calculate, verify, and present for approval before final processing

- Post payroll entries in QBO — journal entries, liability accounts, reconciliation

- Strong written and verbal communication — you will be communicating with our team and sometimes directly with clients

- High attention to detail — payroll errors are expensive and erode client trust

- Reliable and deadline-driven — payroll runs cannot be late, ever



NICE TO HAVE:

- Experience with Gusto (US payroll platform — some of our clients use it)

- PCP designation or equivalent knowledge

- Experience with CRA remittances and year-end T4/T4A filing

- Experience processing ROEs

- Knowledge of Workers Compensation (WorkSafeBC, WCB Alberta)

- Experience with field service companies (construction, trades) where overtime, travel time, and variable hours are common

- Experience with Workiz or similar job management software for pulling time data



WHAT THE DAY-TO-DAY LOOKS LIKE:

- Collect and verify time data from clients (timesheets, apps, exports)

- Calculate regular hours, overtime, stat holiday pay, and any special deductions

- Prepare complete payroll summary for manager review before processing

- Once approved, process payroll and post entries to QBO

- Reconcile payroll liabilities (CPP, EI, tax) in QBO

- Prepare and submit ROEs when employees are terminated or go on leave

- Flag discrepancies — if the numbers do not look right, speak up before processing

- Communicate clearly with the team via Slack/email — ask questions early, do not guess



WE ARE LOOKING FOR SOMEONE WHO:

- Is proactive, not reactive — you catch the problem before it becomes one

- Communicates clearly and often — silence is not golden in payroll

- Takes pride in accuracy — you double-check your own work

- Can manage multiple client payrolls on different schedules without dropping balls

- Is comfortable saying "this does not look right" and asking questions



TO APPLY — Please include:

1. How many years of Canadian payroll experience you have

2. Your experience with QBO payroll specifically (and Gusto if applicable)

3. Describe how you typically prepare a payroll run from start to finish

4. How many clients/companies you have processed payroll for simultaneously

5. Your expected monthly rate

6. Your availability to start

title: Sales Operations Specialist
description:
If you are looking for a mundane, punch-the-clock job, please skip this post.



We are a fast-moving, 3-founder startup (current YC Spring applicants) building Nexeo.ai — an ambitious AI-driven visibility and optimization platform. We are leaving traditional, clunky data dashboards in the past and building an intuitive, blazing-fast, "Apple-level" expert workspace. This is not just a gig: We are looking for a long-term tea
mber who will grow with us, push the limits of what is possible, and build the best possible experiences in today’s world.

We do not care about your age, your years of corporate experience, or your formal degrees. We care about your slope. If you are hungry, detail-obsessed, and driven to build something world-class, we want you.



About the Company:



Angry Digital Inc. is a US-based AI technology company building Nexeo.ai — an AI Search Intelligence platform that helps digital agencies track, measure, and optimize how their clients appear in AI-powered search results (ChatGPT, Google AI Overviews, Perplexity, and more). We’re a fast-moving founding team backed by real revenue from our e-commerce portfolio and owned media properties. This is a ground-floor opportunity at a company building category-defining software.



The Role:



You’ll be the engine behind our sales pipeline. Our Head of Sales needs a sharp, detail-oriented operator who can research prospects, qualify leads, manage CRM data, and ensure every outreach sequence runs like clockwork. You won’t be cold calling or closing deals — you’ll be building the infrastructure that makes closing possible.



Core Responsibilities:



-Source and research prospects using 
, LinkedIn Sales Navigator, and company websites

-Score and qualify leads against our Ideal Customer Profile (ICP) using documented criteria

-Enrich contact records with company size, tech stack, key decision-maker details, and their current situation

-Maintain CRM hygiene in ClickUp — update statuses, log activities, tag leads by stage

-Prepare pre-call research briefs for the Head of Sales before discovery calls

-Monitor inbox replies to outbound sequences and classify response intent (interested, objection, OOO, bounce, not interested)

-Track outbound sequence performance metrics (open rates, reply rates, meeting conversion)

-Assist with drafting personalized
 sequences following approved templates and brand voice

-Maintain and update sales playbook documentation as processes evolve

-Flag high-intent signals (funding rounds, leadership changes, job postings) that indicate buying readiness



Required Skills & Experience:



-2+ years in sales operations, lead generation, or SDR support role

-Proficient with 
 or similar prospecting tools (ZoomInfo, Lusha, Hunter)

-Experience with CRM systems (ClickUp, HubSpot, Salesforce, or Pipedrive)

-Strong English written communication — you will draft research briefs and
 copy

-High attention to detail and ability to follow documented SOPs consistently

-Comfortable working US Eastern Time hours (minimum 4-hour overlap)

-Reliable internet connection and a quiet workspace



Bonus Skills (Not Required):



-Experience in SaaS, digital marketing, or agency environments

-Familiarity with LinkedIn Sales Navigator advanced search

-Experience with AI tools (ChatGPT, Claude) for research acceleration

-Basic understanding of SEO, digital advertising, or e-commerce



Compensation & Details:



-Salary: USD $490.00–$740.00/month depending on experience

-Type: Full-time (40 hours/week)

-Schedule: Monday–Friday, flexible start with minimum 4-hour US ET overlap

-Location: 100% Remote (Philippines-based)

-Benefits: 13th-month pay, performance bonuses tied to pipeline metrics, clear growth path to Sales Operations Manager as we scale



How to Apply:



Send your resume and a short Loom video (under 3 minutes) introducing yourself and walking through one example of a lead research project you completed. Bonus points if your Loom shows your actual screen and tools, not just a talking head.

Applications without a Loom video will not be reviewed.

title: Outbound Sales Closer – Food / FMCG
description:
Read first:



This job is daily cold calling to takeaway shops & grocers in Australia.

If you don’t like cold calling, don’t apply.



What you do

• Call local takeaway shops & grocers

• Offer free samples

• Follow up after sample drops

• Convert the sample drop into a first full-price order

• Hours: Monday–Friday, Australian business hours (approx. 9am–5pm AEST)



Once a shop orders, the account is handed off.

You do not manage reorders or ongoing accounts.



What this is NOT

• No inbound leads

• No account management

• No marketing or strategy work



This is dial>talk >>>convert.



Pay

Commission is paid on adoption, not trials.

• AUD $1,000 monthly base

• AUD $30 one-off commission per converted account

o Commission is paid only when the shop places its first full-price order (2nd order)

o No commission on sample drops or discounted trial orders



Who should apply

• Comfortable making cold calls all day

• Proven experience hitting sales targets

• Can handle rejection

• Clear English

• Reliable and consistent



Sales experience helps. Discipline matters more.



REQUIRED TO APPLY



Applications without an answer will be ignored.



You are given a list of 150 takeaway shops to call in one working day.

How would you structure your calling day to get through the list and book trials?

(5–6 sentences max. Be practical.)

*** Attach your resume with your answer.

title: Content Marketing Operations Specialist
description:
If you are looking for a mundane, punch-the-clock job, please skip this post.



We are a fast-moving, 3-founder startup (current YC Spring applicants) building Nexeo.ai — an ambitious AI-driven visibility and optimization platform. We are leaving traditional, clunky data dashboards in the past and building an intuitive, blazing-fast, “Apple-level” expert workspace. This is not just a gig — we are looking for a long-term tea
mber who will grow with us, push the limits of what is possible, and build the best possible experiences in today’s world.

We do not care about your age, your years of corporate experience, or your formal degrees. We care about your slope. If you are hungry, detail-obsessed, and driven to build something world-class, we want you.



About the Company



Angry Digital Inc. is a US-based AI technology company building Nexeo.ai — an AI Search Intelligence platform for digital agencies. We also operate ATL Rank (
), a business intelligence publication covering Atlanta’s business ecosystem, and produce the Angry Digital Podcast. We’re a fast-moving founding team looking for a content operator who can run our marketing engine daily.

 structured data or JSON-LD

Location: 100% Remote (Philippines-based)

Benefits: 13th-month pay, performance bonuses tied to content KPIs, clear growth path to Marketing Operations Manager as we scale



How to Apply:



Send your resume, a link to 2–3 writing samples (blog posts, articles, or social copy you’ve written), and a short Loom video (under 3 minutes) walking through one content workflow you managed end-to-end — show us your process, not just the output. Bonus points if your Loom shows your actual screen and tools, not just a talking head.

Applications without writing samples and a Loom video will not be reviewed.

title: Junior Accountant (AU Client) – Xero Preferred
description:
Senior Accountant (AU Client) – Xero Certified



Reports to: Managing Director

Location: Remote (Work From Home)

Employment Type: Full Time

Work Schedule: Monday to Friday, 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM Manila Time



About the Role



Staff Connect Australia is recruiting on behalf of an established Australian business operating across multiple service divisions.



We are seeking a detail-oriented Junior Accountant to support the company’s financial operations, reporting, and compliance. This role will work closely with the Managing Director and senior tea
mbers, assisting in day-to-day accounting tasks while ensuring accuracy and timeliness in financial records.



This role is ideal for someone with foundational experience in Australian accounting who is eager to grow their skills in a structured, multi-entity environment.



Systems & Tools Environment

Xero

GeoOp

Live Payments Portal

Airtable

Slack



Certifications Required

- Xero Advisor Certified

- Xero Payroll Certified



Required Experience

- Australian BAS and IAS preparation

- Company tax return support (liaising with external accountants as needed)

- Experience working with Australian clients or accounting firms



Key Responsibilities

Bookkeeping & Reconciliations

-Maintain accurate financial records in Xero for all entities

- Perform bank, credit card, and loan reconciliations

- Allocate GST codes and track job-related costs

- Review and code expense entries with proper documentation

- Reconcile inter-entity transactions and maintain a clean general ledger

-

Accounts Payable (Suppliers & Contractors)

- Process and schedule invoices for payment

- Verify invoice accuracy against purchase orders or job references

- Reconcile supplier statements and maintain positive relationships

- Process weekly contractor payments and maintain payout registers

-

Accounts Receivable (Customer Billing & Collections)

- Review job logs from GeoOp and ensure accurate invoicing

- Identify and correct invoicing/data-entry errors

- Manage debtor follow-ups and resolve invoice disputes

- Maintain an accurate aged receivables ledger and report trends



Payroll & Contractor Management

- Process fortnightly payroll in compliance with Australian award rules

- Audit ove
 claims for accuracy and fairness

- Reconcile PAYG, superannuation, and leave balances

- Maintain employee and contractor payment records

- Post and reconcile all payroll journals



Balance Sheet, Assets & Financial Control

- Maintain reconciled balance sheets each month

- Manage the fixed asset register and depreciation schedules

- Record and monitor capital purchases (vehicles, IT, plant, and equipment)

- Administer dividend payments, director loans, and shareholder distributions

- Track intercompany transactions, retained earnings, and equity movements



BAS / IAS Preparation & Compliance

- Prepare monthly IAS and quarterly BAS for review

- Ensure GST and PAYG data is accurate

- Maintain full working papers and supporting documentation

- Monitor ATO correspondence and ensure timely compliance



Financial & Operational Reporting

- Produce weekly cashflow forecasts and monthly management reports

- Conduct variance analysis and identify cost-saving opportunities

- Track workforce utilization, revenue per labour hour, and service profitability

- Provide actionable insights for management, including pricing and discount analysis



Audit & Year-End Support

- Prepare reconciliations and schedules for audits and EOFY processes

- Liaise with external accountants and auditors

- Assist with year-end adjustments, including depreciation, accruals, and provisions



Key Skills & Qualifications

- Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or equivalent

- Minimum 3+ years’ experience with Australian clients or accounting firms

- Advanced Xero proficiency, including payroll, fixed assets, and reporting

- Strong knowledge of Australian BAS, GST, PAYG, and Superannuation compliance

- Experience managing balance sheets, dividends, and inter-entity accounts

- Excellent accounts payable, receivable, and reconciliation skills

- Strong analytical and financial reporting capability

- High attention to detail and accuracy

- Able to work independently and proactively identify risks or opportunities



Performance Indicators (KPIs)

- Balance sheets reconciled monthly with full supporting schedules

- Zero unexplained variances in payroll, assets, or BAS accounts

- Timely issuance of invoices and active debtor management

- Accurate and punctual supplier and contractor payments

- Management reports delivered with insightful commentary on trends and efficiencies

- Workforce utilization, cancellations, and discount impacts reported monthly

- BAS/IAS submissions prepared ahead of deadlines

- EOFY and audit documentation complete and delivered on time

title: ACCOUNT MANAGER
description:
We are seeking a proactive, client-focused Account Manager who will serve as the primary point of contact for assigned clients. This role is responsible for managing client relationships, ensuring client needs are met, supporting renewals and upsells, and collaborating with internal teams to deliver high-quality service.



The ideal candidate is organized, adaptable, and comfortable handling both client-facing and internal coordination responsibilities.



## Key Responsibilities



**Client Relationship Management**

- Serve as the main liaison between clients and the organization

- Build and maintain strong, long-term client relationships

- Conduct regular client check-ins to review business needs and satisfaction

- Act as the client advocate to ensure expectations are met and exceeded



**Account Support and Retention**

- Welcome and onboard new clients

- Address client concerns and resolve issues in a timely and professional manner

- Track and manage ongoing client requests and deliverables

- Renew existing subscriptions and maintain account continuity



**Revenue Growth and Upselling**

- Identify opportunities to expand services and increase client profitability

- Support upsell initiatives within assigned accounts

- Collaborate with leadership on action plans to drive revenue growth



**Internal Coordination and Support**

- Collaborate with internal departments to fulfill client needs

- Attend meetings, provide updates, and document action items when required

- Support assigned projects and campaigns across departments

- Perform research and administrative tasks as requested by supervisors



**Data and Process Management**

- Collect and analyze client and consumer behavior data

- Maintain accurate client records and documentation

- Track processes related to client requests and outcomes



**Marketing and Communication Support**

- Assist with social media updates and content creation as needed

- Draft basic copy for posts and client communications when required



## Requirements

- High School graduate or equivalent education and or experience

- Strong client-facing and communication skills with a professional demeanor

- Ability to take ownership of client issues and drive them to resolution

- Comfortable working collaboratively across multiple departments

- Strong verbal and written communication skills

- Ability to multitask and manage competing priorities

- Able to perform well under pressure in a fast-paced environment

- Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools

- Advanced social media experience is a plus

- Sales experience of 1 to 2 years is an advantage but not required



If you think you're the one we're looking for, kindly send us your updated CV along with a 1-2min self intro. Thank you!

title: ASSISTANT OPERATIONS MANAGER – CLIENT EXPERIENCE (Female only, Full-Time, Remote – Philippines)
description:
Join a mission-driven company empowering women across Asia to live stronger, healthier, and more confident lives.



We're expanding our operations team — and we're looking for a sharp, polished, and proactive Assistant Operations Manager to be the professional frontline between our clients and our Singapore leadership team.



This is a long-term, full-time position with real career growth for the right person.



YOUR ROLE

You'll work directly with our Operations Manager and Singapore-based Director, supporting the full client journey — from onboarding through to programme completion — while ensuring backend operations run accurately and on time.



Your main responsibilities:

> Manage client onboarding, scheduling, confirmations, and reminders per SOP

> Handle professional client communications via phone, Zoom, and messaging platforms

> Coordinate with Singapore-based coaches and leadership on session logistics and events

> Maintain accurate records across CRM systems, trackers, and shared drives

> Support webinar logistics — registration follow-ups, nurture calls, and attendance confirmation

> Monitor group chats, respond to client queries, and escalate where needed

> Conduct call audits and document client interaction logs

> Uphold confidentiality and professional standards at all times



WHAT YOU'LL LOVE ABOUT WORKING WITH US

- 95% Remote — work from home without the commute

- KPI-based pay — your effort is directly rewarded

- Stable, long-term career role — not a project, not a gig

- Singapore travel opportunity — top performers join us onsite for company events

- Clear SOPs and structured onboarding — you'll never be left guessing

- Leadership that's direct, fair, and invested in your growth

- Training and continuous development — we build you up as we grow

- Integrity-first culture — no politics, no drama



YOU'RE A GREAT FIT IF YOU HAVE:

> Excellent English — written and spoken, polished and professional

> Typing speed of at least 45 WPM

> A warm, confident phone and Zoom presence — clients will speak with you directly

> Strong organisational skills and attention to detail

> Experience in client-facing roles: corporate admin, BPO, hospitality, customer success, or similar

> Proficiency with Google Workspace, CRM tools, and Zoom

> Reliable internet with a backup plan

> A quiet, professional home workspace with webcam and good lighting

> A track record of follow-through, accuracy, and proactive communication



NOT A GOOD FIT IF YOU:

> Have other part-time or freelance commitments

> Are uncomfortable speaking directly with clients on calls

> Struggle with deadlines, SOPs, or working independently

> Need to be reminded to complete recurring tasks

> Respond to challenges with excuses instead of solutions

> Have frequent internet or power disruptions without a contingency plan



We need someone who takes ownership, communicates without being prompted, and holds themselves to a high standard.



WORK HOURS

Tuesday to Saturday | 1:00 PM – 10:00 PM (Singapore Time) Aligned with our Singapore operations and client base.



OUR COMPANY VALUES

Everything we do is built on five non-negotiables:

Honesty — tell the truth, always

Integrity — deliver with excellence and accuracy

Commitment — follow through, every time

Growth Mindset — keep learning, keep improving

Team Spirit — respect, collaboration, and positivity



HOW TO APPLY

Send an

title: Assistant Operations Manager - Remote| Singapore Company
description:
ASSISTANT OPERATIONS MANAGER — REMOTE | SINGAPORE COMPANY

Slim Sassy Strong Pte Ltd | Remote Full-Time | php20K–45K+ | Singapore travel opportunities



You’re good at your job. You’re just tired of the commute.

You’ve spent years working onsite — handling clients, managing workflows, keeping things running. You’re competent, you’re professional, and you show up.

But the traffic is costing you 2–3 hours every day. Hours you’d rather spend building something.

We’re offering you a way out of the commute — without stepping back in your career.



Slim Sassy Strong is a women’s wellness education company headquartered in Singapore. We are hiring one Assistant Operations Manager to join our remote operations team, with a structured path to Operations Manager and Senior Operations Manager.



This is a serious career role, not a work-from-home side gig.



|Phase   |Role                     |Compensation    |

|--------|-------------------------|----------------|

|Month 1 |Training                 |php20,000 fixed   |

|Phase 2 |AOM (KPI-based)          |php28,000 starting|

|Phase 3 |Operations Manager       |php35,000–php45,000 |

|Advanced|Senior Operations Manager|php45,000+        |



Plus: selected tea
mbers travel to Singapore for company events and operations.

Advancement is based on performance — not time served.



You will work directly with Singapore-based leadership as an operational extension of the business:

	-	Manage executive calendars and protect leadership time

	-	Own structured
 communication and client follow-ups

	-	Track KPIs, deadlines, and team task execution

	-	Conduct client-facing calls — professionally and confidently

	-	Support the consult pipeline and marketing-to-ops handover

	-	Maintain SOP compliance and operational documentation



As you grow into OM, you lead the team, own systems, and make decisions independently.

Client communication is a core part of this role — not an occasional task.



Why this works for you:

	-	No commute. Work from home, full-time, with a Singapore company on your resume.

	-	Real career track — AOM ? OM ? SOM with compensation that grows with your scope

	-	Singapore travel — top performers join us onsite for events and operational stints

	-	Above-market ceiling — ?45K+ for senior performers

	-	Long-term stability — we grow with people who perform; we don’t churn talent



You’ll do well here if:

You’ve worked onsite in operations, client coordination, admin, hospitality, or a similar field. You’re used to being relied on. You’re not looking for an easy job — you’re looking for a better setup to do your best work in.

Specifically, you:

	-	Are confident handling clients directly — calls, not just chat

	-	Stay composed and professional under pressure

	-	Meet deadlines without being chased

	-	Communicate clearly and directly, especially when escalating

	-	Are fast with digital tools and willing to learn new systems

	-	Want to build a career, not just collect a paycheck

	-	You don’t mind putting in extra hours when it counts. You just want those hours to actually mean something.



Technical requirements

	-	Minimum 50 WPM typing speed

	-	Strong written and spoken English — business standard

	-	Stable internet with a documented backup connection

	-	Own laptop suitable for business use

	-	Familiarity with Google Workspace or similar tools



How to apply:



 with the exact subject line:

Assistant Operations Manager Application

Include all of the following — incomplete applications will not be reviewed:

	1.	One paragraph: what you’ve handled onsite and why you’re ready to bring that to a remote leadership role

	2.	Your current city and power/internet stability details

	3.	Device specs + internet speed screenshot

	4.	Your backup plan during outages

	5.	A 1–2 minute informal audio introduction

	6.	DISC profile screenshot (OnlineJobs)

	7.	Typing test screenshot (min. 50 WPM)

title: Executive Assistant
description:
Here's the full job description condensed into 3–4 flowing paragraphs:



---



**Virtual Personal Assistant — Part-Time, Remote (8 hrs/week)**



We are looking for a highly organized, proactive, and detail-oriented Virtual Personal Assistant to support an American entrepreneur and consultant based in Berlin, Germany, who operates across multiple time zones and international markets. This is a part-time remote engagement of approximately 8 hours per week, ideal for a self-starter who thrives in an asynchronous global work environment and can manage a diverse mix of tasks with minimal supervision. The role requires fluent English, flexibility across CET and US time zones, and a professional, discreet approach to handling both business and personal matters.



On the business side, the PA will handle executive and administrative support — including
 and calendar management, scheduling, meeting prep, contract review, and copy editing of client-facing documents — alongside travel research and booking, social media scheduling and monitoring, light graphic design in Canva and Google Slides, and finance support using 
, covering invoicing, expense tracking, and monthly reporting.



The role also includes meaningful personal support: vacation and personal travel planning, sourcing and ordering supplies, coordinating in-person errands by liaising with local vendors and service providers, and managing personal appointments and scheduling. Additionally, the PA will assist with data collection and analysis — researching and compiling information, building Google Sheets trackers and dashboards, and preparing concise briefings to support decision-making.



The ideal candidate has proven experience as a virtual PA or executive assistant, strong written English skills including copy editing, solid knowledge of Google Workspace and Canva, comfort with basic bookkeeping, and experience booking complex international travel. Familiarity with 
 and social media scheduling tools is a plus. Above all, we are looking for someone reliable, communicative, and trusted to work independently across a wide range of tasks.

title: E - Commerce Video Editor
description:
Overview

We are seeking an exceptional, data-driven Video Editor obsessed with driving conversions for e-commerce/DTC brands. This is a fast-paced role requiring a marketer's mindset, platform expertise, and rapid iteration based on performance data.



Core Responsibilities (What You Will Do)

Conversion Focus: Edit raw footage (often UGC-style) into scroll-stopping, short-form ads (15-60 seconds) for Meta, TikTok, and YouTube, focused entirely on driving sales.



DR Implementation: Structure videos using Direct Response (DR) frameworks (e.g., Problem-Agitate-Solution), ensuring a powerful hook in the first 3 seconds, and seamless CTA integration.



Technical Execution: Apply advanced post-production techniques: color grading, audio mixing, speed ramping, dynamic transitions, and creating engaging typography/subtitles.



Rapid Iteration: Maintain fast turnaround (24 for first cuts) and produce multiple testing variations based on performance metrics (CTR, ROAS, Hook Rate).



Platform Mastery: Optimize content (9:16, 1:1, etc.) for native feel and best practices across TikTok, Reels, and YouTube Ads.



Pre-requisites (Must-Haves)

Experience: Proven portfolio of successful e-commerce/DTC ad editing (not just cinematic/corporate work).



Software Mastery: Advanced proficiency in Adobe Premiere Pro or Final Cut Pro.



Mindset: Deep understanding of DR Marketing and key metrics (ROAS, CTR). Must be performance-driven, flexible, and willing to iterate based on data without ego.



Skills: Expertise in UGC-style editing, audio/sound design, and closed captioning/subtitles.



Bonus Skills

Proficiency in After Effects, CapCut, or DaVinci Resolve.



Familiarity with ad platforms and marketing metrics.



Workload: Initial paid trial, then transitioning to 10 - 20 hours per week ongoing.



Shift: Flexible hours; work is output-based with strict deadlines.



To Apply:

If you are an exceptional designer with a portfolio that demonstrates world-class DTC brand quality, kindly fill out the application form:






Looking forward to receiving your application!

title: ACCOUNTING TEAM LEAD/HEAD
description:
Qualifications:

Must have Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, Finance or any related Business course

CPA is an advantage but not required

5-10 years of experience in accounting or finance roles, with at least 3 years in a leadership or management position.

Experience in budgeting, financial reporting, and auditing.

Knowledge of tax regulations and compliance with relevant laws.

Strong understanding of financial management, analysis, and forecasting.

Excellent leadership and team management abilities.

Detail-oriented with strong analytical and problem-solving skills.

Proficiency in MS Office, especially Excel.

Ability to manage multiple tasks, prioritize, and meet deadlines.

Strong communication skills for coordinating with internal teams and external stakeholders.

Ability to mentor and lead a team effectively.

High level of integrity and ethics in handling financial matters.



Job Duties



1. Manages overall operations of accounting team



a. Manages tasks and over-all performance of accounting staff members. 

b. Oversees daily activities within the accounting team

c. Oversees work of Accounting Assistant with:

? Billing, follow-up and receipt of payments from clients

? Disbursements to Principals, Suppliers and Service Providers,







2. Performs the following as necessary:



a. Revises draft PO from Sales

b. Reviews accuracy of PO versus BOM, supplier quotation, validates applicable taxes (VAT and Royalty Tax)

c. Prepares bank telegraphic transfer forms Applies for remittance of funds in case of payment to foreign principals.

d. Submits to President for Approval/Signature

e. Purchases dollars for payment to foreign principals f. Prepares checks for disbursement

g. Prepares CVs for President/GM Approval.



3. Coordinates and works closely with other Team Leads to ensure support for business while properly adhering to and implementation of accounting guidelines and processes;



a.Discusses with concerned Account Executive regarding billing issues encountered to ensure prompt collection of payment.



4. Reviews and handles processing of Sales documentation (Deal Registration Form, Bill of Material, Commissions, and Contracts)



a. Reviews and signs-off DRFs and BOMs

b. Ensures completeness of required documentation before endorsement of approval to the President and General Manager

c. Reviews Commission statements of Sales people before endorsement to the

President and General Manager

d. Reviews contracts with customers and service providers regarding terms and conditions. Identifies risk exposure and discusses these with the concerned employee and Management.

e. Directly coordinates with foreign principals

f. Confirms maintenance renewals to Partners coordinated with AEs

g. Communicates with foreign suppliers regarding Tax treaty relief availment h. Requests documents required by BIR in relation to deals/transactions.



5. Prepares all Compensation reports and Government Benefits Remittances:



a. Processes mid-month, month-end, and 13th month pay using company's HRIS and payroll system

b. Prepares Annualization file in preparation for year-end reporting and regular tax of employees

c. Conducts payroll related orientation to new hires and before regularization d. Processes Maternity Leave Pay, Last Pay Computation, and Payout

e. Prepares and processes SSS HDMF PHIC and BIR (1601C) remittance reports and

endorses for President approval of Payment.

f.  Generates BIR form 2316 for employees’ year-end and for those who resigned.

g. Provides annual reports on compensation to Management and dissemination to all EEs

h. Responds to queries on payroll



6. Supervision and review of uploaded/ transmitted accounting documents to

Outsourced Accountants



a.  Clarifies and defines how bookkeepers should handle transactions

b.  Oversees transmittal of accounting documents to external bookkeepers for recording and does transmittal of payroll related documents/entries.

c.  Communicates errors and mis posting to books, pre and post FS reporting

d.  Checks and validates FS reports submitted by bookkeepers versus source documents.

e.  Defines new accounting treatment of transactions for implementation and recording as confirmed by Management.



7. Handles tax and other accounting related referrals to Lawyers and Auditors



a. Handles BIR assessments, prepares all schedules, makes reconciliation to support

NIC/PAN/FAN

b. Does BIR Recon as needed for assessments and consulted with the Lawyers c. Coordinates legal documentation and responses to BIR thru Lawyers

d. Monitors referrals to lawyers made by Accounting (Special projects e.g. Royalty

taxes, Tax Relief, Stock Transfers)

e. Prepares all documentary requirements for submission to BIR (for BIR assessments)

f. Coordinates interim and annual audit of books for yearend financial statements.



8.  Coordinates w Bank Representatives/Managers for company account maintenance;

investments and completion of necessary documents related to the transactions.



a. Obtains updated interest rates for potential additional placements b. Confirms rollover of short-term investments

c. Monitors dollar exchange rate for USD requirement and investment

d. Prepares and submits documentary requirements of account opening, updating, etc, signed by Authorized Signatories.











Periodic Job Duties:



9.  Government Mandated Reports 

a. Prepares and processes



? SSS Contributions/loans remittance form and bank receipt HDMF

contributions/loans remittance form and bank receipt

? PHIC Contributions remittance form and bank receipt

? BIR form 1601C (Withheld taxes on Compensation)

? BIR 1604CF (Annual report of withheld taxes on compensation)

? Alpha list to be submitted to BIR in a CD with EE 2316 soft cop



b.  For Review and Payment Processing



?  BIR 1600, Withholding VAT (for foreign transactions)

?  BIR 1601E, Expanded Withholding tax EWT (for all local purchases)

?  BIR 1601F Final Withholding Tax FWT (for foreign purchases)

?  BIR 2550M/Q Value Added Tax, 12% (Output for Sales minus Input tax for Purchases)

?  BIR 1603 Fringe Benefits Tax (for Supervisory employees)

?  BIR 1702Q Income Tax Return (Based on Revenues, net income)

?  BIR 1702 Annual income tax return (based on Audited FS)

?  BIR 0605 Annual Registration Form

? Accounting Books of Accounts (Sales, Purchases, General Journal, General Ledger) Submits to BIR in a CD or other BIR prescribed format.



10.Other Reports



a. Weekly Cash position report

b.  Update AP and AR schedule for the week

c.   Presents F/S to Mancom



11.Responsible for ensuring that qualified commission payments are paid out as scheduled; reviews commission statements submitted by Sales personnel before endorsement to the Corporate Treasurer and GM.



12.Other Job Duties:



a. Attends in MPR and follows-up action to be done by the Sales people (Sales TO DO)

including outstanding AR for action.

b. Coordination with HR Officer regarding results of Physical Asset Inventory and implementing the necessary adjustment in Lapsing schedule/InfoAsset data to be

used for financial statement.

c. Undertakes special projects related to accounting as may be assigned. d. Meets with Bank reps re: investments and transactions

e. Completed requirements for tax clearance for Philgeps

f. Releases per diem for foreign travel

g. Preparation of Certificate of contribution to Philhealth

h. Validates all scanned and indexed documents (DRF Scanning and Report

Generation Project)

i. Facilitates Budget planning - endorses budget for president approval

j. Documents yearly incentive plan for Sales and other departments, and other accounting procedures for implementation

Edit high-converting video ads for Meta platforms 
 & Instagram)

/Instagram)

title: Senior Recruiter
description:
We are seeking a highly experienced Senior Recruiter to lead and execute full-cycle and strategic recruitment initiatives across a variety of roles within the organization. This role demands not only excellent sourcing and assessment skills, but also a deep understanding of strategic talent acquisition practices—from workforce planning to pipeline development. The Senior Recruiter will partner closely with department heads and senior leadership to build recruitment strategies that align with business goals and support organizational growth.

Key Responsibilities:

Lead and oversee the strategic recruitment process, including proactive talent sourcing, candidate engagement, and workforce planning for both immediate and future hiring needs.

Serve as a consultative partner to department heads and executives, offering insights and solutions to hiring challenges and building long-term recruitment plans.

Conduct job intake meetings with hiring managers to define the ideal candidate profile, assess role criticality, and align on recruitment strategies.

Design and implement targeted sourcing campaigns on platforms like LinkedIn, OnlineJobs, Indeed, and other niche job boards.

Conduct behavioral and structured interviews; assess for both skillset and culture fit.

Review, update, and optimize job descriptions in alignment with market trends and company branding.

Administer hiring assessments and analyze candidate performance against role requirements.

Monitor and report recruitment metrics (e.g., time-to-fill, source effectiveness, conversion rates) to evaluate hiring performance and improve the process.

Mentor and support junior recruiters and recruitment assistants when needed.

Continuously explore and introduce new tools, methods, and channels to enhance recruitment effectiveness and employer branding.



Qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree in Human Resources, Psychology, Behavioral Science, or a related field.

At least 3- 5 years of proven experience in strategic and full-cycle recruitment, preferably in a remote or international setting.

Strong background in sourcing executive, specialist, and volume positions across various departments.





Demonstrated expertise in using ATS (e.g., BambooHR) and sourcing tools (e.g., LinkedIn Recruiter, OnlineJobs.ph, Indeed).

Proficient in Google Workspace (Drive, Sheets, Docs) and digital collaboration tools.

Excellent interpersonal and communication skills; capable of influencing and negotiating with senior stakeholders.

High degree of initiative and autonomy; able to work independently in a fully remote environment.

Analytical mindset with the ability to use data to drive recruitment decisions

Strong sense of discretion and ability to handle sensitive hiring information with confidentiality.
